title: `GET /info` not returning `IndexServerAddress`
body:
### Issue Description

Docker returns the `IndexServerAddress` as a response field from GET `/info`: https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.42/#tag/System/operation/SystemInfo

> Default value: `https://index.docker.io/v1/`. URL of the index server that is used for image search, and as a default for user authentication for Docker Hub and Docker Cloud.

Applications which integrate Docker rely on this field for resolving default registries and auth configuration. Podman currently returns this field as an empty string.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Call `/info` endpoint
2. Observe `IndexServerAddress` is an empty string

### Describe the results you received

`IndexServerAddress` is an empty string

### Describe the results you expected

`IndexServerAddress` should be set to `https://index.docker.io/v1/`

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

`DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/$(id -u)/podman/podman.sock`

comments.body:
> Docker returns the `IndexServerAddress` as a response field from GET `/info`: https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.42/#tag/System/operation/SystemInfo
> 
> > Default value: `https://index.docker.io/v1/`. URL of the index server that is used for image search, and as a default for user authentication for Docker Hub and Docker Cloud.
> 
> Applications which integrate Docker rely on this field for resolving default registries

Applications that work with Podman need adjusting anyway, because the concept of “default registries” works quite differently with Podman. The primary UI is image _aliases_, individually defined for each short name; the secondary (and discouraged!) way is `unqualified-search-registries`, which can contain _multiple_ registries; so there is no right answer that could be written into the `IndexServerAddress` entry.

(I’m not expressing an opinion on whether Podman should write _something_ (and if so, what) into that `IndexServerAddress` field, for compatibility — maybe it should; I’m just trying to point out that the underlying _model_ is different, and that fully matching Podman’s behavior requires taking Podman’s different model into account.)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Should we return the data of the first matching registry?  Only if there is only one registry listed?

Given the
> as a default for user authentication for Docker Hub and Docker Cloud.

Documentation I’d argue we can’t get meaningful compatibility either way.
